name: Adjectives with ma- and Linkers
nameNative: Mga pang-uri na may ma- at pang-angkop
summary: Form descriptive words with the prefix ma-, attach them to nouns, and intensify them naturally.
explanation:
Many Filipino adjectives are built from a root plus the prefix ma-. The root ganda (beauty) becomes maganda (beautiful); bait (kindness) becomes mabait (kind); lakas (strength) becomes malakas (strong). Not every adjective uses ma-, but it is the most common pattern, so spotting it helps you read meaning quickly.

To describe a noun, join the adjective and noun with the linker na or -ng (magandang bahay, beautiful house; mabait na tao, kind person). The adjective can go before or after the noun without changing the meaning.

To say very, you can repeat the root (maganda becomes magandang-maganda, very beautiful) or add sobrang before it (sobrang ganda, so beautiful). For comparisons, use mas (mas maganda, more beautiful) and pinaka- for the superlative (pinakamaganda, most beautiful). These small tools let you describe the world with real colour.
pattern: ma- + root = adjective | adjective + na/-ng + noun | mas / pinaka- for degree
commonMistakes: Dropping the ma- prefix, using ganda alone where maganda is meant.
Forgetting the linker between adjective and noun (maganda bahay instead of magandang bahay).
Saying mas pinakamaganda. Mas and pinaka- do not combine.
Adding ma- to roots that do not take it (some adjectives stand alone).
Using sobra without the linker -ng before the adjective (sobra ganda instead of sobrang ganda).
tip: When you learn a new noun like a quality word, check if its ma- form exists; that is your ready-made adjective.
